fields.label: URL
fields.description: The URL for your GitLab instance (ex: gitlab.domain.com). Leave blank if you use gitlab.com

fields.label: Access token
fields.description: Provide information to grant access to this data source. To learn more about access tokens, [click here.](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/profile/personal_access_tokens.html)

fields.label: Page limit
fields.description: The page limit is the maximum number of pages returned when creating a query. The default is 5.

groups.title: Additional Settings
groups.description: Additional settings are optional settings that can be configured for more control over your data source.

relationships.description: GitLab API connection: the instance base URL (root url, defaulting to https://gitlab.com/api/v4) and the personal access token (secureJsonData.accessToken) that the go-gitlab client sends as the PRIVATE-TOKEN header on every request.

instructions.msg: The GitLab datasource has a single authentication method: a GitLab personal (or project/group) access token stored in secureJsonData.accessToken. The backend copies it from DecryptedSecureJSONData['accessToken'] (pkg/models/settings.go:47) and passes it to go-gitlab's gitlab.NewClient (pkg/gitlab/datasource.go:176), which sends it as the 'PRIVATE-TOKEN: <token>' header on every request (go-gitlab gitlab.go:855-858) — it is NOT an 'Authorization: Bearer' header. The token is required: an empty accessToken makes LoadSettings hard-fail with 'access token can not be blank' (pkg/models/settings.go:48-50).

Minimal working payload: {"url":"https://gitlab.com/api/v4","secureJsonData":{"accessToken":"<gitlab-personal-access-token>"}}. The instance URL lives at the datasource ROOT url (NOT jsonData.url) — the backend reads config.URL and overwrites any jsonData.url (pkg/models/settings.go:30,34). url is optional: when the root url is empty the backend defaults it to https://gitlab.com/api/v4 (pkg/models/settings.go:35-37).

secureJsonData.accessToken is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether the token is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields.accessToken (boolean) instead of secureJsonData.accessToken.

Connection/URL rules: the root url is the GitLab API base URL. go-gitlab appends 'api/v4/' when the URL does not already end with it, so both https://gitlab.example.com and https://gitlab.example.com/api/v4 work for a self-hosted instance (go-gitlab gitlab.go:564-578). The URL MUST include an http/https scheme — a bare host like 'gitlab.domain.com' (as the field tooltip suggests) fails the health check with 'unsupported scheme. Only HTTP and HTTPS are supported' (pkg/plugin/../gitlab/datasource.go:110-111). jsonData.pageLimit (optional, default 5 via pkg/models/settings.go:39-41) caps how many API pages a query fetches.
